channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: "Owenese" Restaurant is using the pavement as a bin, 19th July
item.description:
The restaurant dumps waste outside during the week, sometimes it builds up to the point where the whole pavement is impassible. The foxes also get into it and throw rotting food everywhere.

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown hedges infringing on road lane, 19th July
item.description:
The road outside the nursery is about to become a one way road
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Daly Drive, Bickley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bin next to bus stop needs emptying, 19th July
item.description:
It’s very unsanitary. Disgusting bags of dog excrement flowing out. Not pleasant in hot weather .

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Churchfields Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Car parts and commercial waste, 19th July
item.description:
There are car parts, commercial waste, and abandoned vehicles along the arches on Camberwell station road.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 356, Camberwell Station Road, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Car never moved in two years, 19th July
item.description:
Reported this car a year ago. Guess what, it hasn’t moved and even has a clamp on it. How long does it have to be there to be classed as abandoned? It is also unnecessarily making it more hazardous to left from St George’s onto Bickley Park Road. It also has no MOT and the person advertising their business has renewed road tax for the sole purpose of making it a billboard.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bickley Park Road, Bickley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: AGAIN rubbish dumped, 19th July
item.description:
Please clear Every week I report fly tipped rubbish on council pavement outside this privately owned building of rented out flats on Nunhead Green/Gordon rd The tenants simply throw all their unwanted house stuff onto the council owned public pavement outside next to their home, which creates smells, rats & foxes Why can’t the council ask the dept which regulates private rental Licenses to write to freeholder/owner of this building to tell them his/her tenants are committing an illegal act of flytipping and issue him/her with a fine unless tenants stop. That is the law! ??? I have spoken to many residents & stop owners on the road/green who are all fed up with this flytipping on a regular basis! Ie weekly sometimes every few days. Please can you help? Thank you Smita 07985 618263 Local resident Nunhead

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 29, Nunhead Green, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Huge amount of waste dumped in car park off Bloomfield Road, 19th July
item.description:
Photos taken 19th July 2026

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bloomfield Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned Fridge, 19th July
item.description:
A fridge has been left on the pavement.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mare Street, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Children playing all over construction equipment, 19th July
item.description:
On Saturday there were multiple children (and at least 3-4 parents) playing in the construction site and on the vehicles and equipment. At one point a young girl (4y) was playing on her own in the area and only after I went to get her attention repeatedly did a parent emerge from by the river, at least 50m away. The gates closest to the field were wide open and parents were moving the metal gates around. We are incredibly grateful that this park is being repaired but this is not secure enough when workmen aren’t on site.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Riverside Road, Albany Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside Sainsbury’s on Cavendish Road, 19th July
item.description:
A shop storage trolley and an airport trolley full of rubbish and suitcases left on pavement.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Cavendish Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
